title: Scaffold browser app
overview: Initialize a minimal Vite + TypeScript project that renders a full-screen canvas container with no runtime errors, satisfying all acceptance criteria.

steps.description: Create a Vite project with the vanilla-ts template at the repo root. Generate package.json, tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts, and the default Vite scaffolding.
Run npm install to generate node_modules and package-lock.json.
Replace the current .gitignore with standard Vite/Node ignores: node_modules, dist, .DS_Store, etc.

steps.action: Create index.html with full-screen canvas container
steps.description: Replace the default Vite index.html with a minimal HTML file containing a single root div (#app) that will host the canvas. Include viewport meta, zero-margin body style, and a script tag pointing to src/main.ts.

details.html_structure: <!DOCTYPE html> with lang=en, viewport meta, dark background, 100vw/100vh body with margin:0/overflow:hidden, <div id='app'>, <script type='module' src='/src/main.ts'>

steps.description: Write a minimal main.ts that creates a <canvas> element, sets it to fill the viewport (100vw × 100vh), appends it to #app, and handles window resize. Remove any default Vite boilerplate (counter, CSS imports, etc.).

details.canvas_setup: Create canvas element, set width/height to window.innerWidth/innerHeight, append to #app, listen for resize to update dimensions

steps.description: Create a minimal src/style.css with only a global reset: margin 0, padding 0, overflow hidden, background #000, width/height 100vw/100vh on html and body. Import it in main.ts.
Run npm run dev and confirm the app starts without errors and renders a full-screen canvas.
acceptance_criteria_verified: App starts locally with a single command
A root canvas container is rendered
No runtime errors on first load

steps.description: Run npm run build and confirm it completes without errors. Run npm run preview to sanity-check the built output.

acceptance_criteria_mapping.App starts locally with a single command: npm run dev starts the Vite dev server (step 7)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.A root canvas container is rendered: main.ts creates and appends a full-screen <canvas> to #app (step 5)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Production build succeeds: npm run build completes without errors (step 8)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.No runtime errors on first load: Verified by loading the dev server with no console errors (step 7)

constraints: No cookies, localStorage, or analytics per CLAUDE.md
Keep scaffold minimal — no Three.js, audio, or UI elements yet
risk_notes: Low risk — standard Vite scaffolding with minimal customization.
